behaviors.description: Cron parser rejects invalid expressions (month 13, day 32, malformed syntax) with descriptive errors.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: failure is parser validation/message logic inside a local function boundary. REAL-INFRA: no OS/network/subprocess needed. MOCK-HIDE: mocks are not required; pure inputs catch real failures. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. Adversarial critique: if error text were composed by a separate localization/catalog module, Unit could miss integration defects. Rejection gate: behavior text is strictly about rejecting invalid expressions and descriptive error output, with no external module handoff requirement stated, so higher levels are not required.
behaviors.test_description: Table-driven unit test over invalid cron strings. Assert structured error code and message content (field + reason), including malformed token position when applicable.

behaviors.description: Next-run-time calculator returns correct timestamps for standard cron expressions, including last-day-of-month and nearest-weekday.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: this behavior depends on parser->schedule-object->calculator handoff for semantics like L/W; single-function tests can miss misinterpretation across modules. REAL-INFRA: no real OS/network/subprocess needed. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. Integration-brake checks: (a) no system infra dependency, (b) no AI output dependency, (c) no multi-agent chain, (d) no LLM stage handoff. Adversarial critique: if calculator accepts already-normalized schedule objects and parser semantics are separately guaranteed, Unit could be enough. Rejection gate: behavior explicitly says 'for cron expressions' and includes expression-level edge syntax, which disqualifies pure Unit as minimum.
behaviors.test_description: Integration test wiring real parser + next-run calculator. For each expression/start-time fixture, assert next N timestamps exactly (including month-end rollover and nearest-weekday rules).

behaviors.description: Overlap detector finds schedule pairs firing within a configured minimum gap and reports conflicting windows.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: real failure commonly appears at boundary between generated run windows and overlap logic (inclusive/exclusive boundaries, window construction). Unit-only detector tests may miss upstream/downstream contract mismatches. REAL-INFRA: no OS/network/subprocess needed. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. Integration-brake checks: no trigger for System/Agentic/Workflow. Adversarial critique: if overlap detector consumes primitive timestamp lists and contract is trivial, Unit could be sufficient. Rejection gate: behavior text requires both detection and reported conflict windows from schedules, implying cross-module data handoff, so Integration is the safer minimum.
behaviors.test_description: Integration test with real schedule generation feeding overlap detector. Configure minimum gap (e.g., 5m), assert detected conflicts and exact reported start/end windows for near-boundary cases.

behaviors.description: Timezone-aware scheduling handles DST spring-forward gap and fall-back ambiguity for a 2:30 AM job.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: DST correctness is an interaction between recurrence logic and timezone rules; stubbing timezone behavior can hide real defects. REAL-INFRA: can be reproduced in-process with real timezone library/tzdb; no subprocess or external network required. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. Integration-brake checks: no requirement for System/Agentic/Workflow. Adversarial critique: if timezone conversion is delegated entirely to a single well-tested library wrapper, Unit around that wrapper might catch most bugs. Rejection gate: behavior explicitly targets DST transition semantics (gap/ambiguity), which depend on module interaction with real timezone rules, so Unit is insufficient as minimum.
behaviors.test_description: Integration test using real timezone data (e.g., America/New_York). Assert expected behavior for nonexistent 2:30 during spring-forward and duplicated 2:30 during fall-back, including chosen disambiguation policy.

behaviors.description: If computed next run is already in the past, engine applies missed-run policy (skip vs backfill).
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: failure appears at handoff between next-run computation, policy configuration, and execution planner/state update; pure policy-function unit tests can miss sequencing/state bugs. REAL-INFRA: no mandatory OS/network/subprocess. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. Integration-brake checks: no need to upgrade beyond Integration. Adversarial critique: if the policy decision is a pure function with no state coupling, Unit might be enough. Rejection gate: behavior text says 'engine determines' based on computed run time and configured policy, indicating module orchestration rather than isolated function logic.
behaviors.test_description: Integration test wiring scheduler components with controlled clock inputs and persisted last-run state. Simulate downtime windows and assert whether skipped/backfilled runs and resulting next scheduled time match policy.
